title: Maxing Your Roth IRA Every Year (2010–2025)
description: What maxing your Roth IRA contribution every year from 2010 to 2025, investing entirely in the S&P 500 (SPY), would be worth today — tax-free. This is widely considered one of the most powerful wealth-building tools available to US investors.
tax_note: All growth and withdrawals in a Roth IRA are tax-free in retirement (if qualified). This is not modeled in the dollar figure but is enormously valuable. A taxable account with the same investments would owe capital gains taxes on withdrawal.
income_limit_note: Roth IRA contributions are subject to income limits. In 2024, the phase-out begins at $146,000 (single) and $230,000 (married filing jointly).
educational_note: The Roth IRA is one of the few tax structures that lets gains compound completely tax-free for decades. $91,500 contributed over 16 years grew to ~$195,000 — with zero tax owed on withdrawal in retirement. In a taxable account, federal capital gains tax (15-20%) would be owed on the gains.
